source: Signal taxonomy after Zeitz, The Art and Craft of Problem Solving (strategy -> tactic -> tool). Mappings unvalidated against corpus.

signals.name: Optimal substructure
cue: Answer for the whole input decomposes into answers for
; the same subproblem recurs across choices. Ask:
“if I knew the answer for the first i elements, could I extend it?”

signals.name: Count the ways
cue: “How many ways / distinct sequences / valid strings…”
with an answer too large to enumerate — usually

signals.name: Monotone feasibility
cue: “Minimize the maximum” / “maximize the minimum” / “smallest x such that…”
. If answer x works, every larger (or smaller) x works too — and checking one fixed x is easy.

signals.name: Window validity is monotone
cue: Growing the window pushes validity in
(positive sums only grow; adding chars only adds duplicates) — so the left edge

signals.name: Sortable input
cue: — so you may sort for free.

signals.name: Pair condition
cue: satisfying a sum / difference / ratio condition.

signals.name: Nearest greater / smaller
cue: next / previous greater or smaller element
; “visible” buildings; stock span; trapped water; largest rectangle.

signals.name: Incremental connectivity
cue: “are u and v in the same group?”

signals.name: Min steps, uniform cost
cue: where every move costs the same; states + transitions form an

signals.name: Min-cost path, weighted
cue: . Nonnegative → Dijkstra; negative edges or “at most k edges” → Bellman-Ford.

signals.name: Repeated extreme extraction
cue: Repeatedly take / merge / remove the current min or max
; “k-th largest”; keep best k from a stream; merge k sorted lists.

signals.name: Complement membership
cue: While scanning you only need: has a
(target − x, same key, x seen before)

signals.name: Small n → O(n²) ok
cue: : a quadratic DP or double loop is the

signals.name: Large n → O(n log n)
cue: : rules out quadratic brute force; hints
sort / heap / binary search / one pass

signals.name: Huge numeric answer domain
cue: , but a candidate is quick to verify —
search the answer space, not the array

signals.name: Interval input
cue: : overlap, merging, covering, max non-overlapping selection, meeting rooms.

signals.name: Revocable commitment
cue: at a known cost (“refund a purchase”, “replace an earlier pick”):

signals.name: Digit property over a range
cue: satisfy a property — with L, R up to

signals.name: Grid structure
cue: with cell moves — right/down only → DP; 4-directional → search. Path counts, min path sum, islands.

signals.name: Tree input
cue: (rob houses on a tree, longest path, subtree sums).

signals.name: Subarray as prefix difference
cue: A condition on subarray [l..r] rewrites as a relation between
: sum = k, divisible by k, equal counts of two symbols.

signals.name: Shared-prefix queries
cue: : autocomplete, startsWith, longest common prefix over a dictionary, search against a fixed word set.

signals.name: Two-sequence alignment
cue: compared or transformed into each other: common subsequence, edit distance, interleaving. State is naturally

signals.name: Bulk range updates
cue: ; the final array (or its max) is read

signals.name: Locate in sorted
cue: in an explicitly sorted array; rotated sorted array; peak finding.

signals.name: Capacity, unlimited reuse
cue: Capacity problem where each item is usable

signals.name: Combinable halves
cue: merge of two halves + cross-boundary work

signals.name: Small mode set per step
cue: At each position you're in one of a
(holding / sold / cooldown, k transactions left) with allowed transitions.

signals.name: Range-collapse order matters
cue: Operations act on a contiguous range and the

signals.name: Everything fits the budget
cue: with cheap ops, or total combos ≤ ~1e7) that

signals.name: Enumerate configurations
cue: (permutations, boards) or construct one under hard constraints — needs

signals.name: Connect all, min cost
cue: so all are mutually reachable at

signals.name: All-pairs distances
cue: ; “city with fewest reachable neighbors within threshold”.

signals.name: Probability / expectation
cue: Probability of an outcome or expected value
after random steps (knight on a board, soup servings).

signals.name: Safe local choice
cue: locally best choice provably never hurts
the global optimum — an exchange/swap argument would prove it. Often paired with

signals.name: Sorted 2D matrix
cue: ; search a value or find the

signals.name: Running-quantity constraint
cue: stay valid — or resets when it goes bad

signals.name: Explore reachable region
cue: , mark everything reachable from seeds, detect cycles in a

signals.name: Queries interleaved with updates
cue: — prefix sums go stale; “count smaller after self”.

signals.name: Self-similar nesting
cue: : nested lists, bracketed expressions, encoded strings (

signals.name: Processing order decides cost
cue: of jobs/items and order changes total cost; comparing an
yields a custom sort key (“largest number from concatenation”).

signals.name: Fast/slow pointer signature
cue: detect a cycle, find the middle, or find a duplicate via repeated jumps

signals.name: Operation-complexity contract
cue: names required time complexity for operations

signals.name: Timeline events, not fixed ranges
cue: Points/intervals on a line where you
process starts and ends in sorted order
as you sweep across — distinct from a fixed static interval list.

signals.name: Pattern occurs inside text
cue: all occurrences of a pattern within a larger text
, across strings too long for naive comparison.

signals.name: Balance two running halves
cue: as elements arrive one at a time.

signals.name: Values are a permutation of [1..n]
cue: Array of size n containing values from

signals.name: Only relative order matters
cue: or higher, but the algorithm only needs their

signals.name: n in the 30s–40s
cue: : too big for full 2ⁿ brute force, too small to need a polynomial trick — split the search space in

signals.name: Statement is a literal procedure
cue: step-by-step process to carry out exactly as stated
— no algorithmic insight needed, just careful bookkeeping.

signals.name: Crosses a majority threshold
cue: — guaranteed to exist, or check if it does.

signals.name: Multiple simultaneous sources
cue: starting points spread outward at the same time
(rotting oranges, multi-source fire/infection) rather than a single origin.

signals.name: Number-theory phrasing
cue: GCD, LCM, divisors, coprime, prime factorization, or modular exponentiation

techniques.note: Umbrella. Safe local choice + often sort first; large n rules out DP over choices.
Umbrella for explicit search in sorted data.
XOR/parity structure, or tiny n inviting bit tricks.
Umbrella. Static range aggregates or subarray-to-prefix rewrite.
Exhaustive exploration of a reachable region on grid/graph/tree.
Sorted(able) input + pair/shrink condition; large n forbids nested loops.
Min steps at uniform cost = shortest path in an unweighted state graph.
The constraints alone are the signal.
Contiguous + monotone validity + linear-time budget.
1D position state; extend the answer from previous indices.
Two sequences → (i, j) state; n ≤ 5000 allows O(nm).
Monotone feasibility over a huge numeric answer domain.
Kadane-style: running best-ending-here with reset.
Incremental merging + connectivity queries; DFS can't handle online edge additions.
Order irrelevant → sort, then one safe pass.
Updates interleaved with range queries, at scale.
Must construct configurations, not just count; tiny n.

techniques.t: Binary Search + Two Pointers / Sliding Window
techniques.note: The feasibility check for a candidate answer is itself a window/pointer scan.
Purest prefix-sum case: static array, sum queries.
Counts-only view + safe local adjustment of frequencies.
Grid + restricted moves (right/down) → cell subproblems.
Nearest greater/smaller phrasing is near-deterministic.
DP whose transition is a range sum over previous states — collapse it with prefix sums.
n ≤ 20 + order/assignment subproblems over subsets.
Subarray condition as prefix difference + “seen this prefix value before?”
DP transition = “best previous compatible state”, maintainable in a sorted structure (LIS patience).
Combinable halves with cross-boundary counting.
Shared-prefix queries over a word set.

techniques.t: Binary Search + Data Structure (Segment Tree / BIT / Ordered Set)
techniques.note: Order statistics or feasibility needing a dynamic structure inside the search.
Running balance must stay valid; the greedy reset/choice is provably safe (gas station).
Subtree answers combine at each node.
Positive values make prefix sums sorted → binary search over them.
Dependency phrasing is near-deterministic.
Weighted min-cost path; negative edges or a k-edge cap → Bellman-Ford.
Adversarial turns; state DP over “current player's best”.
Rectangle-sum queries on a grid.
Threshold parameter + “is the graph connected/reachable under it?”
Self-similar nested input mirrors the call structure.
Small named mode set with transitions (stock problems).
Capacity + use-once items; subset-sum phrasing.
Bulk range additions, read once at the end — no tree needed.
XOR's self-inverse property makes range XOR a prefix difference.
Explicitly sorted (possibly rotated) array; locate a value or boundary.
Repeated extreme extraction where the extreme changes dynamically.
Interval input + sort by endpoint + safe local selection.
Digit property over [L, R] with ~1e18 bounds is near-deterministic.
Collapse order matters + n small enough for O(n³).
Count ways + substructure; mod 1e9+7 is the tell.
Two sorted sides matched supply-to-demand (assign cookies, boats).
Choose a processing order; adjacent-swap comparison yields the sort key.
Sorted, ends inward, with a provably safe pairing choice.
Revocable commitments + a heap holding the best undo candidate.
Sorted 2D structure searched directly or via value space.
“Connect all at min cost”; sort edges (Kruskal) + Union-Find.
All-pairs need + small n.
Expected value with recursive step structure.
Capacity + unlimited reuse (coin change).
Fast/slow pointer signature on a linked list or index-as-pointer array.
Named per-operation time complexity on a class you design, not an overall answer.
Timeline events processed in sorted order, not a fixed static interval list.
Pattern-inside-text matching too long for naive O(nm) comparison.
Balance two running halves of a stream (running median) as elements arrive.
Values are a permutation of [1..n]; place-by-index finds the missing/duplicate in O(1) space.
Only relative order matters despite a huge value range — remap to ranks first.
n too big for full brute force, too small to need polynomial time — split and combine.
Statement is a literal step-by-step procedure — implement it directly.
Majority-threshold element, guaranteed or checked, found in O(1) space.
Several simultaneous starting points spreading outward together, not a single origin.
Statement names GCD/LCM/divisors/coprime/modular exponentiation directly.
